title: Polygala bolbothrix
taxonomy: Polygala bolbothrix is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygalaceae. It is a member of the Polygala genus, which consists of approximately 380 species of plants. The Polygala genus is part of the larger order Fabales, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the legume order. This classification is based on the plant's botanical characteristics, such as its leaf structure and flower arrangement. Further classification involves the family Polygalaceae, which is a group of flowering plants that are often herbaceous.

The plant's roots and leaves are used to make infusions and decoctions that are said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. intro: Polygala bolbothrix, also known as Yin-Chen, is a plant species belonging to the Polygalaceae family, characterized by its unique, sprawling habit and small, white or pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory and cardiovascular issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular-protective properties. Bioactive constituents of Polygala bolbothrix include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects.
